name: Newspaper Metaphor
source: Clean Code by Robert C. Martin, Chapter 5: Formatting
problem: Source files are organized in a random or reverse order where low-level details appear before the high-level concepts they support, forcing readers to scroll extensively to understand the overall purpose before seeing it.
steps: Place the most important, highest-level concepts and public API at the top of the file.
Order functions so that each one is followed by those it calls at the next lower abstraction level.
Treat the file like a newspaper: headline (class name/purpose) first, detail last.
Group related functions vertically so the reader can follow the narrative without jumping.
Apply the Stepdown Rule: high-level public methods near the top, private helpers below.
